title: init.ts: command injection via execSync string interpolation
description:
init.ts:63 uses execSync with template literal interpolation to clone repos:

  execSync(`git clone git@github.com:${repo.github}.git ${repoPath}`)

repo.github and repoPath come from user-supplied input. A crafted value like 'my-org/repo; rm -rf /' would execute arbitrary shell commands. Every other git call in the codebase correctly uses execFileSync with an args array.

Fix:
  execFileSync('git', ['clone', `git@github.com:${repo.github}.git`, repoPath]);

title: sync.ts: git push fails on new agent branches with no upstream
start.ts: watcher window sends bare 'camper watch', breaks on local builds
description:
start.ts:24 launches the watcher window with:

  tmux.send(session, 'watcher', 'camper watch')

This requires camper to be installed globally in PATH. A local dev build (npm run build && node dist/bin/camper.js start) will spawn a tmux watcher window that immediately fails because the 'camper' binary is not on PATH.

Fix: use process.argv[1] or the resolved path to the entry point:
  tmux.send(session, 'watcher', `node ${process.argv[1]} watch`);

title: start.ts: coordinator not found causes null dereference crash
description:
start.ts:19 uses a non-null assertion on config.agents.find():

  const coordinator = config.agents.find(a => a.name === config.coordinator)!;

If config.coordinator is misspelled or missing from agents, coordinator is undefined at runtime. Accessing coordinator.name then crashes with 'Cannot read property of undefined' instead of a clear error message.

config.ts already has a getCoordinator() helper that throws a descriptive error — use that instead.

title: init.ts: readline interface not closed if exception thrown during prompts
config.ts: no runtime validation of camper.json structure
description:
loadConfig casts the parsed JSON directly to CamperConfig with no structural checks:

  const raw = JSON.parse(readFileSync(configPath, 'utf-8')) as CamperConfig;

Common misconfigurations — coordinator name not in agents list, agent.repo referencing a key not in repos, reviewedBy naming a non-existent agent — produce confusing runtime errors deep in command handlers rather than a clear 'Invalid config: ...' message at load time.

Add a validateConfig function called from loadConfig that checks:
- config.coordinator exists in config.agents
- every agent.repo (when non-null) exists in config.repos
- reviewedBy/reviews values name real agents

title: sync.ts: QA agent branch ref ignores custom branch field on reviewed agent
start.ts: coordinator cwd uses first repo key instead of coordinator worktree
watcher.ts: bd subprocess errors swallowed silently
compact.ts/clear.ts: unknown agent name silently does nothing
description:
compact.ts and clear.ts both use:

  const targets = agentName
    ? [config.agents.find(a => a.name === agentName)].filter(Boolean)
    : config.agents.filter(...);

When agentName is provided but not found, find() returns undefined, filter(Boolean) drops it, and the loop body never executes. The command exits 0 with no output, giving the user no indication the agent name was wrong.

compare with send.ts which correctly throws: throw new Error(`Unknown agent: ${agentName}`)

Apply the same pattern to compact and clear.

title: sync.ts: worktree list parser breaks on lines with no space (e.g. 'bare')
watcher.ts: floor rounding causes sub-second issue closures to be missed
status.ts: O(n) tmux subprocess spawns for window existence checks
init.ts: invalid repo input during agent definition silently picks first repo
bin/camper.ts: version string hardcoded, will drift from package.json
init.ts: unnecessary dynamic import of readline
tmux.ts: unused execSync import
